rule_id: # ── BLOCK 1: Mutual exclusion — rhythm labels ─────────────────────────────────
# NB gives correct posteriors but all rhythm labels still appear in the output
# list. ME suppresses non-winners so the clinician sees a single primary rhythm.
# Tier-1 labels (VF
rule_type: VT) are NEVER suppressed regardless of Pai.

rule_id: R1
# ── BLOCK 2: Precedence — critical rhythm overrides ──────────────────────────
# If VF or VT is in the output
# clinically irrelevant and dangerous to show alongside a cardiac arrest label.
# NB cannot suppress them from the display — precedence does.
R2a
R2b
R2c
R2d
# ── BLOCK 3: Derived injection — safety net for below-threshold predictions ───
# If a diagnosis is below the 0.10 probability threshold it is filtered out and
# NB never sees it. These rules inject it synthetically when a pattern trigger
# and clinical evidence together make it clinically plausible.
# NOTE: boosting of existing labels was removed — injection only.
# Pericarditis: not predicted by the 15-label model — only reachable via ST_Elevation
R3b
# Unstable_Angina: not predicted by the 15-label model — only reachable via ST_Depression
R4b
# Sgarbossa: LBBB + chest pain should surface STEMI-equivalent even if STEMI < threshold
R7
# Wellens Pattern A: TWI + chest pain should surface NSTEMI even if NSTEMI < threshold
V2
# Torsades de Pointes risk: Long_QT + palpitations → treat as VT-equivalent
V8
